message:
test(testkit): add 5 comprehensive test suites for async runtime parity validation

Add 2288 lines of new integration tests to fcp-testkit covering
async runtime correctness and parity with the fcp-async-core facade:

- cancellation_race.rs (368 lines): Deterministic cancellation-race
  and shutdown-propagation scenarios ensuring proper cleanup of futures
  when parent tasks are cancelled, including nested task trees and
  channel-based coordination under cancellation

- deadline_monotonicity.rs (325 lines): Validates that deadline
  ordering is monotonic across nested timeout scopes, that timer
  resolution is bounded, and that sleep/interval precision meets
  minimum guarantees under the fcp-async-core time facade

- differential_regression.rs (689 lines): Side-by-side comparison of
  tokio vs fcp-async-core behavior for channel semantics, lock
  fairness, task scheduling order, and select! branch priority to
  detect regressions when the facade implementation changes

- logging_contract.rs (381 lines): Verifies that structured logging
  (tracing) integration works correctly with the async runtime,
  including span propagation across task boundaries and enter/exit
  ordering under concurrent workloads

- parity_performance.rs (525 lines): Throughput and latency benchmarks
  comparing fcp-async-core facade operations against direct tokio calls
  to ensure the abstraction layer overhead stays within acceptable bounds

All suites reference bead flywheel_connectors-1ud0u.3.1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
